Hello dear Qase support team,
I enjoy using Qase, thank you.
Here is a list what would make our team use this tool even more productive with:
1. Test cases which have the same milestone version do not add to the test plan with the same milestone version. So, if I add new test cases to the repository after creation test plan with milestone , new test cases must be added manually. But it would be much helpfull if there is an option.
2. At the completed test run I can not filter or select the failed test cases and create new run including them. If I select and press run again it will include all test cases not only selected ones. This is also so helpfull if some test cases fail but some pass and you want only run againg failed ones.
3. Export to xlsx format the test repository bug. In csv format I can  understand the symbols and html code, but in xlsx format I consider not to see the bold. colored or symbolized text formats or code. Because this kind of export is considered to share information with persons so it will not be imported anywhere.
Interface should be simple for main things. It's overloaded with other functions now

column14:
There's already a feature request to have shared steps with child steps; I think this would be an asset both for ease of addition of common tasks and for not creating visual "noise" by having multiple common steps be able to be collapsed. 

Also, I'm hoping you expand the dashboard options. One thing I'd like to have access to is a list of tests that haven't been run after a given number of days for the sake of keeping up with regression testing. I think this could be easily accomplished with a dashboard panel that allowed one to display a selected set of test cases with the last run date. (Disclaimer: we're not using the level of the service with queries, so I don't know if that's one of the things you can already do there.)

Other than that, I'm finding the application intuitive and valuable.

The Repository could use some visual change. Making it easier to identify which suites are on the same level.
And it would be really nice if not all the suites would be displayed all the time at the right hand side. Only the suite I open on the left hand side under Suites.
Thanks.

gherkin mode doesn't allow blank lines, comments or inline screenshots.
test runs don't allow for creating a folder structure for organization. Comments and screenshots entered in a test run are not included in the auto-created Bug report. 
What you call 'Suites' are simply folders and not what QA typically call suites. (Even your View selector calls them folders.) While you need folders to organize you tests as per (e.g.) functional area, suites are used to create a collection of various test cases from different areas for a particular objective (e.g for regression.)

Not asking random survey questions to people whose company chooses what test management software they use.  And who thus have 0 decision making power or reason to recommend to their colleagues the software that the rest of their colleagues are equally required to use already.  And I have no idea whose Saturday game night conversations with friends you think broach the topic of what "hey guess which generic test management software our company decided was the right balance of cheap and functional without any of our inputs or reason to care about it".

So, yeah, that's a 0 on the "Would I recommend it to friends or colleagues scale" not related at all to the quality of the product, but because the possibility of recommending what test management software I'm currently using has a 0% chance to come up among either category of people.

Similarly, my colleagues all also use the same test case software that my company choose to use, there's no reason to recommend (or not) what I have no control over.  I assume this question is meant to be a proxy for something along the lines of "how do you like using our product" or "how does Qase compare to other test case softwares you have used", to which I'd probably rate it around a 7-8/10.  But those aren't the actual question chose to ask, and for that question there is a 0 chance of me having a conversation in which test case software *recommendations* are going to come up, thus the likelihood of me recommending QASE to my friends or colleages is also 0.

Hello,

It would be extremely helpful to have the ability to filter by tags in the Test Cases Automation Ratio widget on the dashboard. Currently, when creating or editing this widget, there is no option to apply tag-based filters, which significantly limits the flexibility of analysis—especially when working with large volumes of test cases categorized by various themes or automation types.

We actively use tags to classify test cases by feature, component, and other relevant categories. Being able to filter by these tags would allow us to more accurately track automation coverage across specific segments of our test suite.

We hope this feature can be considered for inclusion in a future update, as it would greatly enhance the analytical capabilities and overall usability of the dashboard.

Thank you!

column14: There's a few UX issues that make working on a large number of a cases at once difficult, especially when generating a brand new test repository. I've had issues with importing tests not landing in the right hierarchy and there not being a safe way to backup/revert a change which is something that TestRail would let me do. After editing many "ticked" items at once, the selection is retained, which has led to me accidentally building up a large number of items and overwriting previous edits. Shared test report links are great, but the fact that they're genuinely public without any SSO/VPN/credential/password authentication makes them useless to me from a data hygene and security perspective, and the exported PDF reports are not useful at all so I can't use them either and just end up manually writing-up a test report myself. Defects requiring a link to an external system is irritating, and I would rather be able to just input a URL to a defect since we don't yet have Jira connected (also I'm working on multiple Jira projects between our cloud, enterprise, and client Jira databases so this doesn't help). Accidentally entering a wrong result for a test and having to mark it for retest feels annoying, rather than just editing the entered result. It would be nice if I could embed tests from another repository on a read-only basis, so they always reflected the status of the master test (I have a suite of certification tests that I want to apply in multiple projects, but the platform partner might update their cert requirements, meaning I then have to update my master suite and also hunt down every implementation/copy of it in other repositories).
